audits.scope: Columbus-5 core upgrade (Terra Classic chain core modules)
audits.summary: Certik audited the Columbus-5 upgrade and found no critical issues but flagged medium-severity concerns around governance parameter handling and module interactions.

audits.scope: Terra Classic LUNC burn tax mechanism and core module post-collapse patches
audits.summary: Oak Security reviewed post-collapse patches including the 1.2% burn tax implementation and found one high-severity issue related to tax exemption logic that was subsequently patched.

audits.scope: Terra Classic core v2.x chain upgrade modules
audits.summary: SCV Security reviewed the v2 chain upgrade and found no critical vulnerabilities; medium findings related to IBC module parameter validation were addressed before mainnet deployment.

exploit_history.description: The UST algorithmic stablecoin death spiral: coordinated large UST sells on Curve depleted the 4pool, triggering a bank run. The LUNA/UST mint-burn arbitrage mechanism hyperinflated LUNA supply from ~350M to ~6.5 trillion tokens in days, collapsing both UST and LUNA to near zero. Approximately $40B in market cap was destroyed. This was a fundamental design failure of the algorithmic stablecoin model, not a smart contract exploit in the traditional sense.

Terra Classic chain halted multiple times during the collapse period due to governance attacks and validator coordination failures. The chain was halted by validators to prevent further damage, representing a liveness failure and partial censorship event.

A vulnerability in the LUNC burn tax exemption whitelist logic was discovered post-deployment, allowing certain contract interactions to bypass the 1.2% burn tax. Patched in subsequent chain upgrade v2.1.1. No direct funds stolen but tax revenue was lost during the window.

code_health.bug_bounty_platform: Immunefi (Terra Classic listing — verify current status at immunefi.com/bounty/terraclassic)

dependencies.provider: Terra Classic Native Oracle (validator-reported price feeds)
dependencies.notes: Price feeds are reported by the active validator set (~130 validators). Oracle manipulation or validator collusion is a known risk; the oracle was implicated in the UST depeg as price feeds lagged during the crisis. No external oracle like Chainlink is used.

Terra Classic uses IBC for cross-chain transfers. IBC itself has a reasonable security track record, but LUNC's low liquidity and validator set quality increase relayer and light-client risk. Wormhole bridge was previously used and suffered a $320M exploit on Solana (unrelated to LUNC directly).

Terra Classic is built on Cosmos SDK v0.46.x with Tendermint BFT consensus. The chain runs a modified/forked version that may lag upstream security patches. Cosmos SDK has had historical vulnerabilities (e.g., Dragonberry IBC exploit 2022) that required rapid patching across all Cosmos chains.

Chain upgrades and parameter changes are controlled by governance votes weighted by staked LUNC. The validator set is relatively small and concentrated; a coordinated 33%+ stake could halt the chain, and 67%+ could pass malicious governance proposals. Community governance has been contentious and slow post-collapse.

rationale: Terra Classic carries extreme legacy risk from the May 2022 UST/LUNA collapse — the largest DeFi loss event in history (~$40B) — which was a fundamental protocol design failure rather than a conventional smart contract exploit, making it uniquely catastrophic. Post-collapse, the chain is maintained by a small, volunteer-heavy developer community with low commit velocity (~2 commits/week) and relies on a concentrated validator set for both consensus and oracle price feeds, creating persistent centralization and governance attack vectors. While post-collapse audits (Oak Security, SCV) have addressed specific patch vulnerabilities, the overall security posture remains weak due to thin developer resources, a high-risk native oracle design, and the reputational/liquidity fragility that makes the chain a continued target.
